discovery.run_summary: Risk-off tape with semis leading the decline (SMH -3.7%, NVDA/AVGO/ORCL sharp drops) while defensives (XLP +2.8%, XLV +2.2%, XLRE +2.0%) and regional banks (KRE +2.8%) catch a bid. VIX up ~9% to 17 on Alphabet Gemini delay news and semi weakness. Rotation out of AI/tech into defensives is the dominant theme; mid-cap semis and defense-tech names are getting hit in sympathy.

notable_index_moves: SMH -3.7% leading decline; semi equipment (ONTO, AEIS, VECO) all down 4-7% with no company news
XLP +2.8%, XLV +2.2%, XLRE +2.0% — classic defensive rotation
KRE +2.8% regional banks bid on likely rate/curve read after Fed's Logan comments
GOOGL -4.4% on Gemini 3.5 Pro delay report — likely driver of broader AI-adjacent weakness

discoveries.name: CareDx, Inc.
discoveries.setup: Large single-day spike with no 8-K and earnings still two weeks out
discoveries.thesis: A ~35% move on ~4x volume with no filings and no news in the packet points to either a leaked/reported positive datapoint (Medicare coverage decision, competitor setback, or M&A chatter) that isn't in our feed, or a squeeze. Without the driver visible, direction of the fade/follow-through cannot be committed.
discoveries.what_confirms: News of a favorable LCD/reimbursement decision on kidney transplant surveillance testing, or a competitor (Natera) regulatory/legal setback would justify the magnitude and suggest follow-through
discoveries.what_kills: If the move is purely short-squeeze mechanics with no fundamental catalyst, expect give-back into earnings
discoveries.catalyst: Unknown from our feed; earnings 14 days out so not a pre-print
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: catalyst_signals shows only upcoming earnings on 7/30 — nothing explains the magnitude
research_pointers: Check for Medicare LCD updates on AlloSure/transplant surveillance
Check Natera (NTRA) newsflow for a peer-negative event
Check short interest and options flow for squeeze signature
Scan for M&A rumor reports on CareDx

discoveries.name: Chart Industries, Inc.
discoveries.setup: Deal-close 8-K bundle (2.01 + 3.01 + 3.03 + 5.01 + 5.02 + 5.03) — classic M&A completion signature
discoveries.thesis: The 8-K item combination — completion of disposition/acquisition, delisting notice, change in control, officer changes, charter amendments — is the exact fingerprint of a merger closing. Stock flat on 3x volume is consistent with arb unwind at deal price, not a fundamental reaction. Not a mispricing; it's a mechanical event.
discoveries.what_confirms: 8-K exhibits confirm completion of the previously announced merger and delisting of common stock
discoveries.what_kills: If the 8-K is actually a spin-off or divestiture rather than a full merger close, the read changes and the flat print could mask a real reaction
discoveries.what_to_learn: Item combo 2.01 + 3.01 + 5.01 + 5.03 in a single 8-K is a near-certain merger-close signature; flat price on huge volume is arb unwind, not signal
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: 3.01 delisting + 5.01 change in control + 5.03 charter amendments filed together is the standard closing-day bundle
research_pointers: Confirm which counterparty acquired GTLS and at what terms
If holders receive stock in acquirer, identify the acquirer ticker for downstream analysis

discoveries.name: Onto Innovation Inc.
discoveries.setup: Semi equipment name selling off in sympathy with SMH -3.7% on light volume with no company-specific catalyst
discoveries.thesis: ONTO is down ~7.5% on 0.65x average volume with no 8-K and no news — that volume profile suggests indiscriminate SMH/semi-cap-eq selling rather than company-specific de-rating. TSM print was actually reassuring on AI capex; the sympathy dump on light volume in a defensive-rotation tape looks like an overshoot into an already weak 5-day base.
discoveries.what_confirms: Continued semi weakness without ONTO-specific bad news, then reversal when tape stabilizes; peer AEIS/VECO also down on light volume supports sympathy read
discoveries.what_kills: An 8-K appears late showing a customer cancellation, guide-down, or negative pre-announcement — then the light volume is misleading and the move is fundamental
discoveries.what_to_learn: Sympathy dumps on sub-1x volume in a broadly-weak sector often overshoot; the tell is the volume signature vs peers with actual news
discoveries.catalyst: Sector sympathy with semi weakness; no company-specific news detected
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Empty catalyst_signals plus 0.65x volume is inconsistent with a company-specific rerating
research_pointers: Check if any semi-cap-eq analyst downgrades hit today
Compare ONTO's move vs KLAC/LRCX/AMAT to gauge whether it's leading or lagging peers
Watch for post-close 8-K filings — the light volume is the key tell

discoveries.name: Kratos Defense & Security Solutions
discoveries.setup: Defense-tech name down sharply on 0.53x volume with ITA -2.3% and no company-specific catalyst
discoveries.thesis: KTOS down ~5.5% on roughly half its average volume while defense ETF is down ~2.3% and there's no 8-K. AVAV (direct peer in unmanned) is UP 5.7% today — that divergence within the same sub-sector on a no-news day suggests KTOS is being sold by a specific holder or on a stale analyst note rather than a real fundamental development. Light volume plus peer divergence is the classic overshoot signature.
discoveries.what_confirms: AVAV holding gains while KTOS keeps sliding without news, then reversal; or an analyst note surfacing that explains a temporary rotation between the two names
discoveries.what_kills: An 8-K or Pentagon news item surfaces showing a specific Kratos program loss or contract issue
discoveries.catalyst: Sector weakness (ITA -2.3%) plus intra-sector rotation; no company-specific catalyst detected
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Empty catalyst_signals, 0.53x volume, and AVAV moving opposite direction all suggest non-fundamental selling
research_pointers: Check if AVAV/KTOS pair-trade rotation is being driven by an analyst note
Check congressional defense authorization headlines for program-specific mentions

discoveries.name: Beam Therapeutics Inc.
discoveries.setup: Gene-editing peer down sharply into a weak XBI tape with CRSP also down
discoveries.thesis: BEAM and CRSP both down 5-8% with XBI -2.7% — the gene-editing subgroup is being hit but there's no visible catalyst. Could be a peer clinical readout, a policy concern, or just biotech risk-off. Without knowing whether there's a specific readout/data event driving it, cannot commit direction; the 5-day move (-21%) suggests something specific is going on that isn't in our packet.
discoveries.what_confirms: A specific negative datapoint (competitor readout, FDA action, or program discontinuation) surfaces that justifies the multi-day slide
discoveries.what_kills: If the slide is purely tape/rotation, expect stabilization once XBI base builds; if specific bad news exists, expect follow-through
discoveries.catalyst: Unknown — no 8-K, no news in feed; XBI weakness is a partial factor but doesn't explain the multi-day magnitude
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Empty catalyst_signals but -21% over 5 days is too large for pure sector drift
research_pointers: Search for recent gene-editing clinical setbacks (VERV, EDIT, CRSP program updates)
Check for any Vertex partnership commentary that affects the CRSP/BEAM combined outlook
Look for FDA policy statements on base/prime editing

ai_announcements.headline: Microsoft training salespeople to pitch in-house models over OpenAI/Anthropic
ai_announcements.summary: MSFT positioning its own models as cheaper/more efficient. IS Anthropic-related — competitive threat to Anthropic (and OpenAI) enterprise business. Flagging for bias-check: consensus interpretation is that MSFT pulling back from OpenAI/Anthropic reliance is a real competitive/margin threat, not something to downplay.

ai_analysis.no_signals_note: None of today's AI announcements have a clear mechanistic link to the specific stocks that moved in the market_data. The big movers appear driven by non-AI catalysts: CDNA +35.6% (biotech/diagnostics news, earnings in 14d — not AI); BEAM/CRSP -5 to -8% (gene editing sector weakness, unrelated to AI news flow); ONTO/LSCC/VECO/AEIS down 4-7% (semi-equipment/analog semi weakness — could be tangentially AI-capex-related but no announcement today explains the move, more likely sympathy to a sector datapoint or ASML/TSM read-through not in this news set); MRCY/KTOS down and AVAV up (defense rotation, unrelated); GTLS flat on 3x volume with a heavy 8-K (M&A/delisting items) — that's the story, not AI; SAIA/TXRH/UFPI/FIZZ/WEX/BMI/MANH up 4-5% (pre-earnings positioning or sector rotation). Forcing an AI narrative onto these would be manufacturing signals. Michael's edge today is NOT here — recommend passing. Watch semi-equipment names (ONTO, LSCC, AEIS, VECO) for a real AI-capex catalyst; if they continue lower on no news, could set up as OVERDONE candidates once a catalyst emerges.
